sourceMovie.title: Knocked Up
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Knocked Up (2007) stuck because it commits to its comedy premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Judd Apatow's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The comedy and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Juno
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Knocked Up clicked for you, Juno (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and romance, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Knocked Up?
faq.answer: Try Juno, Bridget Jones's Diary, The Switch—each shares DNA with Knocked Up while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Knocked Up?
faq.answer: Knocked Up (2007) is best described as Comedy, Romance, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Knocked Up part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Knocked Up Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
